title: Refine AI Cockpit Vision Around Calibrated Human-Agent Trust

sources.reason: Defines the mandatory AI Cockpit Work Item workflow and finish criteria.
Current English product positioning and primary entry-page structure.
Authoritative design philosophy to be refined around calibrated trust.
Defines the current governance architecture and component boundaries.
Defines current product positioning, principles, and evolution model.
Defines authoritative documentation roles and navigation.
Defines repository terminology and architectural boundaries.

problemStatement: Current documentation accurately describes AI Cockpit as a repository governance layer, but it does not clearly distinguish the project's mission from its mechanism. Product positioning has accumulated governance, release, supply-chain, and maintenance concepts, increasing cognitive cost and making the Core boundary less clear.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: Mission, philosophy, mechanism, and implementation are consistently distinguished across authoritative documentation.
scenarioCoverage.reason: The final documentation presents Mission, Philosophy, Mechanism, and Implementation in a consistent hierarchy.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: English, Japanese, and Chinese README entry pages preserve equivalent positioning semantics.
evidence: Final README ordering assertions passed for README.md, README.ja.md, and README.zh-CN.md
Side-by-side review of the three README entry pages
scenarioCoverage.reason: The three entry pages now share the same problem → Mission → Calibrated Trust → product boundary → non-goals → philosophy/mechanism → evidence boundary → demo order.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: Native governance evidence and delegated domain evidence remain clearly separated without changing runtime behavior.
scenarioCoverage.reason: Native Governance Evidence and Delegated Domain Evidence remain distinct, with no schema or runtime changes.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: Release evidence is not presented as a Core tool implementation, while project-owned release maintenance remains accurately described.
scenarioCoverage.reason: SBOM is treated as an evidence artifact, CycloneDX as a standard/format, and named tools as external implementations.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: No code, test, CI, Makefile, schema, guard, installer, or release-flow file is modified.
scenarioCoverage.reason: Only Contract-declared documentation, governance records, and generated Status paths are changed.

riskAssessment.reason: The change spans authoritative positioning documents and three language entry pages. Inconsistent terminology could create conflicting product boundaries even though runtime behavior is unchanged.

executionDecision.reason: The user has approved the documentation-only objective, terminology, file scope, non-goals, and acceptance criteria.

preReviewWarnings: Review whether calibrated trust is described as appropriate reliance and intervention rather than increased trust.
Review all three README languages for semantic consistency.
Review whether SBOM and release tooling remain external evidence-producing capabilities rather than Core implementations.
Review the final diff for accidental runtime, schema, Makefile, CI, or release-flow changes.

checkpointPolicy.reason: Record at least one checkpoint before finishing to reduce mid-task drift.

acceptance: README.md states that AI Cockpit enables calibrated trust between humans and AI agents through evidence-based governance.
README.md continues to define the concrete product boundary as a Repository Governance Layer for AI-assisted software development.
The English, Japanese, and Chinese README files express equivalent Mission, Philosophy, Mechanism, and product-boundary semantics.
The design philosophy defines calibrated trust as evidence-based reliance and intervention rather than maximizing trust in agents.

The exact principle 'AI Cockpit governs evidence; it does not replace evidence-producing tools.' appears in docs/philosophy/design-philosophy.md, and README.md presents the same responsibility boundary without contradictory wording.
The documentation distinguishes native governance evidence from delegated domain evidence without introducing new schemas or runtime concepts.
SBOM, vulnerability scanning, provenance, and signatures are described as replaceable domain or release evidence rather than Core implementations.
Tests and coverage are not described as exclusively release-stage evidence.
The roadmap defines a Core admission criterion and permits consolidation, replacement, or removal of concepts when governance guarantees are preserved.
The documentation architecture keeps README as a short entry point and assigns detailed philosophy, architecture, and reference content to their authoritative pages.
No code, tests, CI, Makefile, schema, guard, installer, distribution, or release behavior changes are included.
Documentation metadata and all Contract-declared AI governance checks pass.
guidelines: Keep terminology language-neutral, project-neutral, and consistent across authoritative documents.
Distinguish Mission, Philosophy, Mechanism, and Implementation.
Do not overclaim current HCI scope or existing runtime capabilities.
Prefer replacing or consolidating duplicate README content over appending new sections.
Preserve existing architecture and lifecycle semantics.
Record no personal paths, credentials, or machine-specific configuration.

intent.problem: Current documentation accurately describes AI Cockpit as a repository governance layer, but it does not clearly distinguish the project's mission from its mechanism. Product positioning has accumulated governance, release, supply-chain, and maintenance concepts, increasing cognitive cost and making the Core boundary less clear.
constraints: This Work Item is documentation-only and must not change runtime behavior.
Keep the current product scope limited to human-agent collaboration in software repositories.
Describe calibrated trust as evidence-based reliance and intervention, not as maximizing trust in agents.
Keep Repository Governance Layer and Evidence Governance as implementation mechanisms.
Distinguish native governance evidence from delegated domain evidence.
Do not describe SBOM or specialized release tooling as AI Cockpit Core capabilities.
Do not claim that AI Cockpit produces no evidence, because it creates governance records.
Do not mechanically replace every occurrence of AI Governance.
Do not add a new Vision document or expand the README with duplicated theory.
Keep English, Japanese, and Chinese entry-page semantics aligned.
intent.rationale: A mission-mechanism distinction preserves the existing governance architecture while establishing calibrated human-agent trust as the long-term North Star. Updating authoritative documentation before behavioral changes prevents premature restructuring and provides a stable boundary for later Work Items.

restrictedWriteApproval.reason: The user explicitly approved .ai/glossary.md as an in-scope target document for this documentation-only Work Item.

rollbackNote: Revert this Work Item diff and restore related tests and docs.
